title: All uses of the #pragma directive should be documented
description: All uses of the #pragma directive should be documentedAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode Smellbased-on-misracertWhy is this an issue?More InfoThe#pragmadirective is implementation-defined, hence it is important both to demonstrate that all uses are correct, and to minimize, localize and encapsulate any use of pragmas within dedicated functions whenever possible.The meaning of each pragma shall be documented.There shall be sufficient supporting description to demonstrate that the behavior of the pragma and its implications for the application, have been fully understood.This rule flags all instances of#pragmadirectives, and leaves it to the user to determine whether they have been properly documented.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: #pragma warning(disable: 4996)     // Missing explanation of why disabled
compliant:
// Set structure packing to 1 byte for network protocol compatibility
#pragma pack(1)

// Disable deprecated function warning for legacy code compatibility
#pragma warning(disable: 4996)

// Use pragma once for header guard to prevent multiple inclusions
#pragma once

checks.message: Pragma directive detected. Ensure it is properly documented with explanation of behavior and implications.
